days.title: Arrival in Tokyo
days.subtitle: First encounters in the bustling metropolis

timeline.title: Arrive at Narita International Airport
timeline.detail: Clear immigration and customs before heading to your accommodation in Tokyo. Follow the signs for the Keisei Skyliner train.

timeline.title: Keisei Skyliner to Ueno Station
timeline.detail: Enjoy a comfortable 41-minute ride into Tokyo city center via the Skyliner. The views along the way showcase the transition from rural to urban.

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Check in to this iconic hotel known for its Godzilla theme, conveniently located just a 5-minute walk from Shinjuku Station.

timeline.title: Lunch at Ichiran Ramen
timeline.detail: Savor world-famous tonkotsu ramen and enjoy the unique solo dining booths. Be ready for an interactive ordering experience!

timeline.title: Explore Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
timeline.detail: Stroll through this tranquil garden featuring cherry blossoms, ponds, and traditional Japanese landscapes. Perfect for relaxation amidst the hustle.

timeline.title: Dinner at Omoide Yokocho
timeline.detail: Experience the charm of this narrow alley lined with small izakayas serving grilled skewers and delicious local dishes. Enjoy the vibrant atmosphere.

localTips: Get a Suica or Pasmo card at the airport for easy train travel in Tokyo.
Most restaurants in Shinjuku don’t allow reservations – expect to queue.
Use Google Maps or Citymapper for navigation – very reliable for public transport.

days.title: Exploring Tokyo
days.subtitle: Immerse in the rich culture and bustling streets

timeline.title: Breakfast at Sarabeth's
timeline.detail: Enjoy a hearty breakfast featuring pancakes and eggs benedict in this popular New York-based restaurant in Shinjuku. A great way to fuel your day.

timeline.title: Visit Senso-ji Temple
timeline.detail: Explore Tokyo's oldest temple, Senso-ji. Wander through the Nakamise shopping street leading up to the temple, offering traditional snacks and souvenirs.

timeline.title: Lunch at Uobei Shibuya Dogenzaka
timeline.detail: Experience a fun, fast-paced sushi conveyor belt lunch. Choose from a variety of options via a touchscreen menu — great for sushi lovers.

timeline.title: Shop at Takeshita Street
timeline.detail: Stroll down the trendy Takeshita Street in Harajuku, filled with colorful fashion shops, trendy cafés, and unique boutiques that showcase youth culture.

timeline.title: Tokyo Tower
timeline.detail: Head to the iconic Tokyo Tower for panoramic city views. As the sun sets, observe the dazzling city lights flicker to life — the perfect photo opportunity.

timeline.title: Dinner at Gonpachi Nishi-Azabu
timeline.detail: Dine in a restaurant famous for inspiring the 'Kill Bill' set. Enjoy a variety of Japanese dishes in a vibrant atmosphere with live cooking.

localTips: Try the melon pan at Nakamise Street near Senso-ji.
For discounts, check out the 100 yen shops around Takeshita.
Avoid peak hours at Tokyo Tower for shorter lines.

days.title: Day Trip to Nikko
days.subtitle: Exploring natural wonders and UNESCO sites

timeline.title: Breakfast at the hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a delicious breakfast at the hotel to prepare for an exciting day trip. Try local rice and miso soup.

timeline.title: Take the Limited Express train to Nikko
timeline.detail: Travel by Limited Express train from Shinjuku Station to Nikko. The journey takes about 2 hours, offering scenic views of the mountains.

timeline.title: Arrive in Nikko and visit Toshogu Shrine
timeline.detail: Explore the intricately carved Toshogu Shrine, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and the final resting place of Tokugawa Ieyasu, the founder of the Tokugawa shogunate.

timeline.title: Lunch at a local soba restaurant
timeline.detail: Experience traditional soba noodles at a local restaurant nearby. Nikko is famous for its delicious, handmade soba dishes.

timeline.title: Visit Kegon Falls
timeline.detail: Head to beautiful Kegon Falls, where you can view one of Japan's most famous waterfalls. Take the observation platform for the best view.

timeline.title: Return to Tokyo
timeline.detail: Head back to Tokyo after a full day of exploration. Enjoy the scenic train ride back as the sun sets.

timeline.title: Dinner at Sukiyabashi Jiro
timeline.detail: Experience world-renowned sushi at Sukiyabashi Jiro in Ginza, famed for its meticulous craft and dedication to sushi perfection. Reservations are essential.

localTips: Make a reservation for Jiro at least a month in advance.
The train to Nikko can get crowded; reserve seats if possible.
Bring a camera for Kegon Falls — it's incredibly photogenic.

days.title: Last Day in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Last-minute shopping and departure

timeline.title: Breakfast at the hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy your final breakfast at the hotel. Consider trying Japanese pancakes for a sweet treat!

timeline.title: Explore Shibuya Crossing
timeline.detail: Visit the iconic Shibuya Crossing, one of the busiest pedestrian crossings in the world. Capture the energy of Tokyo here with bustling crowds.

timeline.title: Lunch at Shake Shack Shibuya
timeline.detail: Enjoy a delicious burger at Shake Shack, known for its quality beef and lively atmosphere located just steps from Shibuya Crossing.

timeline.title: Last-minute shopping at Harajuku
timeline.detail: Return to Harajuku to pick up some unique souvenirs and clothing from trendy shops on Takeshita Street, ensuring you leave with memorable items.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel and pack
timeline.detail: Return to the hotel to pack your bags and prepare for checking out. Make sure you have all your souvenirs and travel essentials.

timeline.title: Travel to Narita International Airport
timeline.detail: Leave the hotel and head to Narita Airport via the Narita Express train from Shinjuku Station, ensuring you arrive in plenty of time for your flight.

localTips: Use Google Maps for navigating the train schedules; it’s user-friendly.
Shibuya's famous Hachiko Statue is just steps from the crossing; be sure to check it out.
Arrive at the airport at least 3 hours before your flight for international departures.

hotels.name: Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
hotels.note: 5-min walk from Shinjuku Station, Godzilla-themed hotel.

budget.item: Nikkan Shuzai Soba for lunch in Nikko
budget.note: Lunch at a soba restaurant, widely acclaimed in Nikko.
Fine dining sushi experience; reservations recommended.
Famous ramen place with a unique dining experience.
Dinner at the iconic restaurant inspired Kill Bill.
Fun sushi conveyor belt dining experience.
Metro passes and convenience store snacks throughout the trip.

urgent.label: Book Jiro reservation
urgent.note: Reservations at Sukiyabashi Jiro are essential; book in advance.
